hypothesis: Training a multi-fidelity surrogate model that predicts both energy above hull (stability) and DFT-relaxation RMSD directly from generated crystal structures, using a combination of unrelaxed coordinates, symmetry descriptors, and low-cost lattice features, will match full DFT screening accuracy (AUC ≥ 0.9 for stability; RMSD error ≤ 0.1 Å) on held-out MP/OQMD samples while reducing required DFT evaluations by at least 75%, enabling scalable online screening in the evaluation framework.
